mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals resilient household demand and supports near-term revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS is rising.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value was 705180 as of two months before 2024-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if periods with positive 3-month RSAFS changes do not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods with negative 3-month RSAFS changes, or if the walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 5.0%, restrictive policy raises discount rates and tightens financial conditions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value was 5.33 as of one month before 2024-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 5.0% have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or have an average return not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate alongside continued payroll expansion identifies late-cycle labor cooling, where earnings risk rises before employment fully contracts, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3pp over 3 months while PAYEMS is still above its 6-month average
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 4.0 as of one month before 2024-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered late-cycle labor-cooling observations deliver positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the interaction term UNRATE change times PAYEMS trend has nonnegative predictive coefficient in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in industrial production signals improving real output and operating leverage, increasing expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value was 102.7623 as of two months before 2024-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO changes are followed by lower average 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative 3-month INDPRO changes, or if the sign of the predictive coefficient is negative in more than half of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1400 thousand units, residential investment weakness signals slowing durable-goods demand and credit sensitivity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value was 1360.0 as of two months before 2024-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if observations after HOUST crosses below 1400 have 60-day S&P 500 returns above the unconditional average or if the negative signal fails to achieve a hit rate above 52% in walk-forward classification.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation acceleration combined with a high federal funds rate creates a stagflationary policy constraint, keeping discount rates elevated while pressuring margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change exceeds its 12-month change while FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value was 313.225 as of one month before 2024-06-15
fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value was 5.33 as of one month before 2024-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-inflation high-rate trigger is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of instances or if adding the interaction does not improve out-of-sample log loss versus separate CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS effects.

mechanism_narrative: Positive real GDP growth combined with unemployment at or below 4.2% identifies a soft-landing expansion in which earnings growth offsets discount-rate pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter change is positive and UNRATE is at or below 4.2%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value was 22749.846 as of five months before 2024-06-15
fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 4.0 as of one month before 2024-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered soft-landing observations have average 60-day S&P 500 returns below the unconditional average or if the GDPC1 growth and low-UNRATE interaction has a nonpositive coefficient in walk-forward regressions.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.0%, labor-market slack begins to erode household income expectations and raises recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 4.0 as of one month before 2024-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if UNRATE crossings above 4.0% are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the average triggered return is not below the unconditional 5-day average.
